title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government provides its response to a report on artificial intelligence and public standards, outlining its position on the committee's recommendations. The document aims to address the key findings and suggestions put forth in the original report on AI and public standards.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems, exploring potential copyright implications and their consequences. The report examines the intersection of copyright law and AI to provide insights for stakeholders in the tech and creative industries.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The UK Department for Education's AI policy document outlines their position on using generative AI in education. The document likely addresses the department's stance on its educational applications and potential implications.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan aims to leverage the benefits of artificial intelligence in the transport sector while minimizing potential risks. It describes how the Department for Transport (DfT) will collaborate with industry stakeholders to achieve this goal.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document is a progress report discussing the use of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems, likely outlining current practices and any necessary regulations.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: This document outlines the government's response to recommendations on regulating artificial intelligence used in medical devices. The government is addressing and implementing guidelines based on the Regulatory Horizons Council's report on AI as a medical device.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's policy on regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications, responding to government requests for guidance. The policy aims to ensure AI is used safely and effectively in qualifications, without compromising their credibility or integrity.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has provided a response to the House of Lords Select Committee's recommendations regarding artificial intelligence. The response outlines the government's plans and actions to address the concerns and findings presented in the 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency' report.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing how artificial intelligence interacts with public standards. The committee will likely examine the current state of AI and public standards, identifying areas for improvement.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has made a submission to a parliamentary inquiry on how artificial intelligence should be governed. The submission provides written evidence to inform this inquiry and shape policies surrounding AI.
